concerns.text: The plan relies on a 'Verified Datasets' block containing only irrelevant data (text, traffic, medical) and proposes using 'synthetic data' to validate the pipeline. The specification (FR-001, FR-002) explicitly requires ingesting 'publicly available metabolomic datasets from NCBI GEO, Zenodo' with paired pre-stress/post-stress metrics. Synthetic data cannot validate the specific data ingestion, normalization, and biological plausibility logic (FR-008, FR-012) required for real metabolomic profiles. The plan fails to identify a real, accessible plant metabolomic dataset that meets the pairing criteria, rendering the core research question unanswerable with the proposed resources.

The plan states the approach involves 'ingesting raw data from public repositories' but the accompanying research analysis confirms no such data exists in the verified list. Proceeding with synthetic data for the 'research_complete' phase violates the assumption that public datasets contain the necessary paired recovery metrics. The pipeline logic for handling real-world missing data (>10% rejection) and KEGG mapping of real metabolite names cannot be validated with synthetic inputs.
